title: Collabium balansae
intro: Collabium balansae, also known as Balansa's Collabium, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may aid in the treatment of various health issues, such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Collabium balansae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Collabium balansae is often used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as being dried and made into capsules or tablets.
taxonomy: Collabium balansae is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, it is classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is further divided into the tribe Malaxideae and the genus Collabium. As part of the Malaxideae tribe, Collabium balansae is closely related to other orchid species within this group.
